title: Password Reset Flow Update
briefing: The account team wants to release an updated password reset flow. QA says the happy path works, but the release manager wants one last risk review before deployment.

introducedIssues.summary: The reset token expiry was increased to 24 hours, which deviates from the safe baseline expectation for short-lived reset tokens.
The change only received smoke testing even though account recovery changes should receive stronger regression testing.
Prior unsolicited reset-email reports were not clearly reviewed before the release.

evidenceCards.title: QA Smoke Test Note
evidenceCards.body: The reset link works when requested by the account owner. Login succeeds after changing the password. No full regression test was completed because the change was considered small.

evidenceCards.title: Developer Comment
evidenceCards.body: The token expiry value was temporarily increased to 24 hours to reduce support complaints. We can reduce it later if needed.

attachments.title: Reset token configuration

evidenceCards.title: Support Ticket Summary
evidenceCards.body: Several users previously reported receiving password reset emails they did not request. Support marked the issue as low priority because no confirmed account takeover was reported.

evidenceCards.title: Release Note Draft
evidenceCards.body: Improves password reset reliability and user experience. No user-facing security changes are mentioned in the draft release note.

expectedFindings.summary: The password reset token expiry was increased to 24 hours, which may increase account takeover risk if reset emails are accessed by someone else.
The change only received smoke testing even though it affects an account security flow.
Previous unsolicited password reset emails suggest the release should be reviewed carefully before shipping.

authoring.notes: First MVP ticket used to prove the content pipeline and first playable release-review loop.
